questions.question: Why are efficient means of transport necessary for a country’s development?
questions.answer: Efficient transport helps move goods and people quickly, promoting trade and economic growth.

questions.question: Why are transport and communication complementary to each other?
questions.answer: Transport moves goods and people while communication transfers information, together supporting trade and development.

questions.question: Give two advantages of road transport over railways.
questions.answer: Road transport is cheaper to construct and provides door-to-door services.

questions.question: What is the main aim of the Golden Quadrilateral project?
questions.answer: Its aim is to reduce travel time and distance between major cities of India.

questions.question: What are metalled roads?
questions.answer: Metalled roads are roads made of materials like cement, concrete, or bitumen and can be used in all seasons.

questions.question: Why are border roads important?
questions.answer: Border roads improve connectivity in remote border areas and support national defense.

questions.question: What role do railways play in the national economy?
questions.answer: Railways transport passengers and goods across long distances, supporting trade and economic development.

questions.question: Why are railway lines difficult to construct in the Himalayan region?
questions.answer: The Himalayan region has steep slopes, high relief, and sparse population, making railway construction difficult.

questions.question: What is pipeline transport?
questions.answer: Pipeline transport is a system used to transport liquids like crude oil, petroleum products, and natural gas.

questions.question: State one advantage of pipeline transport.
questions.answer: It reduces trans-shipment losses and delays during transportation.

questions.question: Why are waterways considered fuel-efficient?
questions.answer: Waterways use less fuel compared to road or air transport when carrying heavy goods.

questions.question: Name any two National Waterways of India.
questions.answer: Ganga (NW-1) and Brahmaputra (NW-2).

questions.question: Why are ports important for international trade?
questions.answer: Ports act as gateways for imports and exports between countries.

questions.question: Why was Kandla port developed after independence?
questions.answer: Kandla port was developed to reduce the pressure on Mumbai port after the loss of Karachi port.

questions.question: Why is air transport useful in remote areas?
questions.answer: Air transport can easily reach difficult terrains like mountains, deserts, and forests.

questions.question: What is the aim of the UDAN scheme?
questions.answer: The UDAN scheme aims to make air travel affordable and improve regional connectivity.

questions.question: What is mass communication?
questions.answer: Mass communication is the spread of information to a large audience through media like radio, TV, and newspapers.

questions.question: Name two examples of mass communication.
questions.answer: Radio and television.

questions.question: What is international trade?
questions.answer: International trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries.

questions.question: What is favourable balance of trade?
questions.answer: It occurs when the value of exports exceeds the value of imports.

questions.question: Name two commodities exported by India.
questions.answer: Gems and jewellery, and chemicals.

questions.question: Name two commodities imported by India.
questions.answer: Petroleum crude and electronic goods.

questions.question: How does tourism promote national integration?
questions.answer: Tourism brings people from different regions together and promotes cultural understanding.

questions.question: Name two types of tourism in India.
questions.answer: Heritage tourism and eco-tourism.

questions.question: How does tourism support local handicrafts?
questions.answer: Tourists buy local handicrafts and products, helping local artisans earn income.
